meta.title: TJC IV Room Inspection Readiness — Compliance Matrix
meta.subtitle: Survey-aligned checkpoints mapped to USP <797> (2023 Revised), USP <800>, and current TJC standards
primarySources: USP General Chapter <797> Pharmaceutical Compounding — Sterile Preparations (2023 Revised, official 2023-11-01)
USP General Chapter <800> Hazardous Drugs — Handling in Healthcare Settings (effective 2019-12-01)
USP General Chapter <795> (2023 Revised) — referenced for nonsterile-to-sterile
TJC Comprehensive Accreditation Manual — Medication Management (MM) and Environment of Care (EC) chapters
meta.validationNote: Verify TJC standard numbers against your facility's current edition; standards renumber between accreditation cycles.

modules.title: Facility Design & Environmental Controls
modules.tag: Air quality · pressure · ISO classifications · PEC/SEC

keyMetrics.label: ISO 5 (PEC)
keyMetrics.value: ≤3,520 particles/m³ at ≥0.5 µm · ≤29 particles/m³ at ≥5 µm

keyMetrics.label: ISO 7 (buffer)
keyMetrics.value: ≤352,000 particles/m³ at ≥0.5 µm

keyMetrics.label: ISO 8 (anteroom)
keyMetrics.value: ≤3,520,000 particles/m³ at ≥0.5 µm

keyMetrics.label: ISO 7 ACPH
keyMetrics.value: ≥30 total · ≥15 from HEPA-filtered supply

keyMetrics.label: ISO 8 ACPH
keyMetrics.value: ≥20 total

keyMetrics.label: Buffer pressure (non-HD)
keyMetrics.value: ≥+0.020 in WC vs anteroom (positive)

keyMetrics.label: C-SEC pressure (HD)
keyMetrics.value: −0.01 to −0.03 in WC (negative)

keyMetrics.label: Cert frequency
keyMetrics.value: Every 6 months by qualified Certifying Organization

checkpoints.objective: ISO classification verified by current particle-count certification
checkpoints.criterion: Certificate dated within 6 months on file; counts at ISO 5 ≤3,520/m³ at 0.5 µm and ≤29/m³ at 5 µm; ISO 7 ≤352,000/m³; ISO 8 ≤3,520,000/m³

checkpoints.objective: Buffer-room ACPH and HEPA contribution meet minima
checkpoints.criterion: ISO 7 buffer ≥30 ACPH total with ≥15 ACPH from HEPA-filtered supply; ISO 8 anteroom ≥20 ACPH

checkpoints.objective: Continuous pressure-differential monitoring with alarms
checkpoints.criterion: Buffer room ≥+0.020 in WC vs anteroom (non-HD); C-SEC HD buffer −0.01 to −0.03 in WC; documented daily review with audible/visual alarm on excursion

checkpoints.objective: Temperature and humidity controls within tolerances
checkpoints.criterion: Buffer ≤20 °C and ≤60 % RH; refrigerator 2–8 °C; freezer −25 to −10 °C; logs reviewed and signed daily

checkpoints.objective: HD facility separation and external venting
checkpoints.criterion: C-PEC for HD compounding is BSC II Type B2 or CACI externally vented (no recirculation); HD storage in dedicated negative-pressure room or in negative C-SEC if low-volume; segregated from non-HD active pharmaceuticals

keyMetrics.label: Garbing order
keyMetrics.value: Outer items off → shoe covers → hair/beard cover → mask → 30-s wash → gown → alcohol hand-rub → sterile gloves LAST → sanitize gloves with sterile 70% IPA on entering PEC

keyMetrics.label: Initial qualification (Cat 1/2)
keyMetrics.value: 3 separate passing events: garbing observation + GFS + media fill

keyMetrics.label: Initial qualification (Cat 3)
keyMetrics.value: All Cat 1/2 + media fill under most stressful conditions + endotoxin/sterility expertise verification

keyMetrics.label: GFS / media-fill (Cat 1/2)
keyMetrics.value: Every 6 months

keyMetrics.label: GFS / media-fill (Cat 3)
keyMetrics.value: Every 3 months

keyMetrics.label: Garbing observation (Cat 1/2)
keyMetrics.value: Every 12 months

keyMetrics.label: Garbing observation (Cat 3)
keyMetrics.value: Every 6 months

keyMetrics.label: GFS action level
keyMetrics.value: Initial: any growth = fail · Ongoing: >3 CFU per hand → action-level excursion

keyMetrics.label: Media-fill action
keyMetrics.value: Any growth in any container after 14-day incubation at 20–25 °C and 30–35 °C = immediate suspension

keyMetrics.label: USP 800 training
keyMetrics.value: Required for ALL HD handlers (pharmacy, nursing, receiving, transport) — refresh every 12 months

checkpoints.objective: Each compounder's initial qualification packet is complete and immediately retrievable
checkpoints.criterion: On file for every compounder: garbing observation + 3 separate passing GFS + 3 separate passing media fills + didactic record + PIC sign-off; retrievable by name within minutes
evidence: Pull a random name and produce within 5 minutes

checkpoints.objective: Ongoing competency cadence matches category
checkpoints.criterion: Cat 1/2: GFS + media fill every 6 months · garbing every 12 months. Cat 3: every 3 months / 6 months. HD personnel: USP 800 retraining at minimum every 12 months and after any HD-list or facility change.
evidence: Most recent results within frequency window

checkpoints.objective: Garbing sequence performance matches written SOP
checkpoints.criterion: Direct observation matches the USP 797 garbing order; sterile gloves donned LAST; gloves sanitized with sterile 70% IPA upon entering PEC; observed by trained assessor (no self-assessment)
evidence: Observation checklist with assessor signature and date

checkpoints.objective: GFS and media-fill action-level excursions are investigated and documented
checkpoints.criterion: Any GFS >3 CFU/hand or media-fill growth triggers documented suspension, root-cause investigation, retraining, and 3 consecutive passing re-qualifications before resuming independent compounding

checkpoints.objective: USP 800 training extends beyond pharmacy
checkpoints.criterion: Training records on file for ALL personnel handling HDs — receiving, materials management, pharmacy, nursing, environmental services, transport — with annual refresher and current NIOSH-list comparison
evidence: Current HD list reviewed within 12 months

keyMetrics.label: Sequence (CSP)
keyMetrics.value: Clean → Disinfect → Apply Sporicidal · always cleanest → dirtiest, top → bottom

keyMetrics.label: Sequence (HD)
keyMetrics.value: Deactivate → Decontaminate → Clean → Disinfect · order is NOT interchangeable

keyMetrics.label: Inside ISO 5 / ISO 7
keyMetrics.value: Sterile 70% IPA only — non-sterile IPA is non-compliant

keyMetrics.label: PEC frequency
keyMetrics.value: Start of shift · before each batch · every 30 min of continuous use · after contamination · end of shift

keyMetrics.label: Sporicidal
keyMetrics.value: Monthly minimum (Cat 1/2) · Weekly (Cat 3 and HD areas)

keyMetrics.label: Buffer + anteroom daily
keyMetrics.value: Floors · work surfaces · sinks · walls within 1 m of work surface · equipment exteriors

keyMetrics.label: Buffer + anteroom monthly
keyMetrics.value: Walls (full) · ceilings · doors · light fixtures · storage shelving

keyMetrics.label: Pass-through
keyMetrics.value: Both surfaces wiped with sterile 70% IPA between every transfer · daily full clean · monthly sporicidal

keyMetrics.label: Wiping supplies
keyMetrics.value: Sterile, low-lint, single-use; one direction only with overlapping strokes

keyMetrics.label: Dwell time
keyMetrics.value: Disinfectant must remain visibly wet for the full manufacturer contact time (most-missed step in surveys)

checkpoints.objective: Sterile 70% IPA used inside ISO 5 and ISO 7
checkpoints.criterion: All IPA spray bottles or wipes inside classified areas labeled 'Sterile' with lot number and use-by; non-sterile IPA never enters ISO 5/ISO 7
evidence: Container labels at point of use

checkpoints.objective: Sporicidal-agent rotation and documented monthly application
checkpoints.criterion: Sporicidal applied at least monthly (weekly Cat 3 and HD); rotation schedule prevents resistance; agent, date, lot, and personnel documented for every application
evidence: Cleaning log with monthly sporicidal entries

checkpoints.objective: Dwell-time observance during direct observation
checkpoints.criterion: Disinfectant remains visibly wet for the full manufacturer contact time; staff state the dwell time for each agent in use
evidence: Posted dwell-time reference at point of use

checkpoints.objective: Cleaning sequence — clean before disinfect, cleanest to dirtiest
checkpoints.criterion: Cleaning physically removes debris BEFORE disinfectant applied; direction PEC outward (PEC → buffer → anteroom); SOP explicit; staff demonstrate correct order

checkpoints.objective: HD areas follow Deactivate → Decontaminate → Clean → Disinfect
checkpoints.criterion: Each of the four steps performed in order with appropriate agents; deactivator residue removed before disinfectant applied; performed at start of shift, after every HD batch, after spills, and end of shift

modules.title: Hazardous Drug (HD) Handling — USP <800>
modules.tag: C-PEC/C-SEC · CSTDs · spill kits · PPE · transport

keyMetrics.label: NIOSH groups
keyMetrics.value: Group 1 antineoplastics · Group 2 non-antineoplastics meeting NIOSH criteria · Group 3 reproductive-only

keyMetrics.label: C-PEC
keyMetrics.value: BSC Class II Type B2 (100 % HEPA-exhausted to outside) OR CACI externally vented

keyMetrics.label: C-SEC
keyMetrics.value: ISO 7 with negative pressure −0.01 to −0.03 in WC · ≥30 ACPH · externally vented (no recirculation)

keyMetrics.label: C-SCA alternative
keyMetrics.value: Permitted for low-volume HD compounding · LIMITS all HD CSPs to maximum 12-hour BUD

keyMetrics.label: CSTD compounding
keyMetrics.value: RECOMMENDED in C-PEC where chemical/physical compatibility allows

keyMetrics.label: CSTD administration
keyMetrics.value: REQUIRED for antineoplastic HD administration when dosage form allows

keyMetrics.label: Compounding PPE
keyMetrics.value: Two pairs chemo gloves (ASTM D6978) · chemo gown (ASTM F739 or equivalent) · two pairs shoe covers · hair, beard, mask · eye/face protection · ≥N95 (P100 for volatile HDs)

keyMetrics.label: Glove change
keyMetrics.value: Every 30 minutes or sooner if torn / contaminated

keyMetrics.label: Gown change
keyMetrics.value: Every 2–3 hours or sooner if contaminated · single-use only

keyMetrics.label: Spill kits
keyMetrics.value: At every HD touchpoint — receiving, storage, prep, transport, administration

keyMetrics.label: Spill > 5 mL
keyMetrics.value: Trained spill responders only · consider P100 PAPR · evacuate non-essential staff

keyMetrics.label: Medical surveillance
keyMetrics.value: Baseline + every 2–3 years for significant HD handlers · post-exposure evaluation triggered by spill / splash events

checkpoints.objective: Current NIOSH HD list adopted and assessment of risk on file
checkpoints.criterion: Facility's HD list reviewed against current NIOSH publication within 12 months; written assessment of risk (AOR) on file for any drugs handled in alternative containment
evidence: AOR document signed by P&T or pharmacy leadership
NIOSH list version on file with comparison date

checkpoints.objective: C-PEC externally vented and certified
checkpoints.criterion: BSC II Type B2 or CACI externally vented (no recirculation); certification within 6 months; airflow alarm functional; HEPA pre-filter change-out documented per manufacturer

checkpoints.objective: CSTDs used during administration of antineoplastics
checkpoints.criterion: Direct observation: CSTD on every antineoplastic line; closed-system maintained from spike to administration; staff explain the closed-system principle and identify CSTD failure modes

checkpoints.objective: Spill kits at all HD touchpoints with named, trained responders
checkpoints.criterion: Spill kits within reach at every HD receiving, storage, prep, transport, and administration location; contents intact and within expiry; named, trained spill-response personnel posted on each shift

checkpoints.objective: Medical surveillance program in place for HD handlers
checkpoints.criterion: Baseline health questionnaire on file at hire; reassessment every 2–3 years; post-exposure medical evaluation triggered for spill or splash events; records secured per HIPAA

keyMetrics.label: Immediate-use
keyMetrics.value: ≤4 h administration window from prep start (USP 797 2023 — extended from 1 h legacy) · single patient/single dose · ≤3 sterile components · ≤2 entries per container · no HDs

keyMetrics.label: Category 1
keyMetrics.value: ISO 5 PEC in unclassified SCA · 12 h CRT / 24 h refrigerated · no frozen

keyMetrics.label: Category 2
keyMetrics.value: ISO 5 in ISO 7 + ISO 8 cleanroom · BUD per method × component status × storage × sterility test

keyMetrics.label: Category 3 (NEW 2023)
keyMetrics.value: Cleanroom + enhanced controls · sterility + endotoxin + container-closure integrity + stability data · personnel q3 mo · max BUDs aqueous 60/90/120 d, nonaqueous 180/180

keyMetrics.label: Label required
keyMetrics.value: Patient ID · drug + amount · diluent + volume · route · storage · BUD (date AND time) · facility · lot · auxiliary labeling

keyMetrics.label: Pharmacist quality release
keyMetrics.value: Verifies labeling · identity · quantity · integrity · BUD · documentation BEFORE every dispense

keyMetrics.label: Documentation retention
keyMetrics.value: Minimum 3 years (state may require longer — apply more stringent standard)

checkpoints.objective: BUD policy reflects USP 797 (2023 revised) including Category 3
checkpoints.criterion: Facility policy explicitly references Category 1, 2, AND 3; immediate-use window stated as ≤4 hours from prep start; BUD tables match the 2023 framework
evidence: BUD reference table at point of use

checkpoints.objective: BUDs assigned in Compounding Records match category and storage
checkpoints.criterion: Random sample of 10 recent CRs: BUD recorded matches the category, storage condition, and (if applicable) sterility-test status. No Category 2 or 3 BUDs assigned to CSPs prepared in an SCA.

checkpoints.objective: Every CSP label contains all required elements
checkpoints.criterion: Random sample of 5 dispensed CSPs: labels contain patient ID · drug + amount · diluent + volume · route · storage · BUD with date AND time · facility · lot · applicable auxiliary labeling
evidence: Direct sampling on the dispensing line

checkpoints.objective: Medication security maintained throughout IV room and storage
checkpoints.criterion: All medications inaccessible to unauthorized personnel; controlled substances per DEA + state; HD storage segregated and labeled; high-alert and LASA separation per facility policy; refrigerator/freezer access restricted with continuous temp monitoring and alarming

checkpoints.objective: Pharmacist quality release before every dispense
checkpoints.criterion: CR shows pharmacist signature confirming labeling, identity, quantity, integrity, BUD, and documentation completeness BEFORE the CSP is released. No CSP released without all five verifications.
evidence: CR samples with verification fields populated

tjcCrossReference.topic: HVAC, pressure differentials, humidity, temperature in cleanrooms
